Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kyle J. Clawson of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, the insurer of defendant Associated Insulation of California, Inc., a suspended corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert James Hanson

RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

At the Johnston (#274950) hearing the Court granted parties' request to also argue the same motions in the Lujan (#275496), Hanson (#275624), Liberty (#275871), Turner (#275910), and Harig (#276181) cases. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as to the Hanson case: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on August 15, 2012 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on January 21, 2016 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) as well as the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kristi Okumoto of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Blue Diamond Growers
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert James Hanson

RULING:
DEFENDANT BLUE DIAMOND GROWERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Blue Diamond Growers' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense would apply to bar plaintiff's premises owner/contractor liability claim based on failure to warn. The court cannot summarily adjudicate plaintiff's claims based on failure to warn because defendant failed to specify that it sought adjudication of this issue of duty in its Notice of Motion. Therefore defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense is a complete bar to plaintiff's claims that negates any duty defendant may have owed to Mr. Hanson. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages if off calendar as plaintiff has dismissed such claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert James Hanson

RULING:
DEFENDANT WASHINGTON GROUP INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Washington Group International Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to the statute of limitations issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Under the analysis of Uram v. Abex Corporation (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425, plaintiff submitted evidence creating a triable issue. Plaintiff's various depositions and declaration and Dr. Raybin's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff's breathing problems were caused by asbestos exposure. Further, plaintiff submitted evidence of a triable issue as to whether he knew or in the exercise of reasonable diligence that he should have known the causes or contributing factors of his disability. Since the "uncontradicted facts" do not have only one legitimate inference, summary judgment is not proper. (Jolly v. Eli Lilly and Company (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar May-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael E. Sandgren of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Lawson Mechanical Contractors
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Robert James Hanson

RULING:
DEFENDANT LAWSON MECHANICAL CONTRACTORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Lawson Mechanical Contractors' motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the statute of limitations issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Under the analysis of Uram v. Abex Corporation (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425, plaintiff submitted evidence creating a triable issue. Plaintiff's various depositions and declaration and Dr. Raybin's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff's breathing problems were caused by asbestos exposure. Further, plaintiff submitted evidence of a triable issue as to whether he knew or in the exercise of reasonable diligence that he should have known the causes or contributing factors of his disability. Since the "uncontradicted facts" do not have only one legitimate inference, summary judgment is not proper. (Jolly v. Eli Lilly and Company (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103.) As to the causation issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to the retained control argument, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff's premises owner/contractor liability cause of action is not viable even if plaintiff cannot establish that defendant retained control over any of his jobsites. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Campbell Soup Company'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents; Requests For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Robert James Hanson
James J. O'Brien, Esq., of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / opposing party Campbell Soup Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFF ROBERT HANSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel the depositions of defendant Campbell Soup Company's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for production of documents is denied. In light of the fact that defendant's PMK was recently deposed on some of the issues covered by the deposition notice and plaintiff's own deposition has not concluded, plaintiff has not provided good cause for its motion at the present time. Defendant's cross-motion for protective order is granted only to the extent that the motion seeks to defer the deposition sought by plaintiff until a reasonable time after the deposition of plaintiff has concluded. All requests for sanctions are denied. No party acted without substantial justification. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
